SEARCH
Package server.util

Class ServerLog

java.lang.Object
server.util.ServerLog
public final class ServerLog extends Object
Minimal server-side error logging — a thin, consistent wrapper over System.err so a failure always shows up in the server console as one located line plus its stack trace, rather than vanishing.

Deliberately not a logging framework: the project carries no logging dependency and the server already routes activity through ServerListener. This helper covers the two places that cannot reach a listener — the server.dao DAOs (no listener handle) and any other server-side catch that would otherwise swallow a throwable. The per-request guard in OrderServer pairs a error(String, Throwable) call (full stack to the console) with a listener.onError(...) line (the red entry in the GUI activity log).

Both methods write to System.err — the same stream Throwable.printStackTrace() uses — so the located line and its trace stay together in the console.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
    private
    ServerLog()
    Non-instantiable: all logging methods are static.

    Modifier and Type

    Description

    The frame that called daoError(Throwable).

    Logs a DAO failure as [DAO] <Class>.<method> failed: <message> followed by the stack trace.

  • Constructor Details

    • ServerLog

      private ServerLog()
      Non-instantiable: all logging methods are static.

  • Method Details

    • error

      public static void error(String context, Throwable t)
      Logs a located error: prints "<context>: <message>" followed by the full stack trace to System.err.
      Parameters:
      context - human-readable location/operation (e.g. an op + session, or a [DAO] Class.method failed tag)
      t - the failure to report

    • daoError

      public static void daoError(Throwable t)
      Logs a DAO failure as [DAO] <Class>.<method> failed: <message> followed by the stack trace. The class and method are derived from the calling frame so the line stays correct even if the method is later renamed. Call it directly from a DAO catch block: ServerLog.daoError(e);
      Parameters:
      t - the SQL (or other) failure caught in the DAO

    • callerFrame

      private static StackTraceElement callerFrame()
      The frame that called daoError(Throwable). Index 0 is this method, 1 is daoError, so 2 is the DAO method that caught the exception.
      Returns:
      the DAO caller's stack frame, or null if unavailable

    • simpleName

      private static String simpleName(String className)
      Strips the package prefix from a fully-qualified class name.
      Parameters:
      className - the fully-qualified class name
      Returns:
      the simple (unqualified) class name

    • message

      private static String message(Throwable t)
      A non-null message for t, falling back to its type when blank.
      Parameters:
      t - the throwable
      Returns:
      its message, or its simple type name if the message is null/blank
